rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WISFE AISH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Release Of Mechanics Liens
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER WISFE AISH, WA-SFCT LLC, 288 27TH STREET LLC, A&M PROPERTIES, L.P. Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Release Of Mechanics Liens is OFF CALENDAR, moot. Releases recorded on September 17, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALEX SAJOKIVC VS. SARA WILLIAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders Compelling Plaintiff (1) To Install The Interior Chairlift Per Code And Without Further Delay (2) To Provide Actual Measurements Showing Proposed Chairlift Installation Complies With Dbi Requirement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT SARA WILLIAMS, MARIA WILLIAMS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders Compelling Plaintiff (1) To Install The Interior Chairlift Per Code And Without Further Delay (2) To Provide Actual Measurements Showing Proposed Chairlift Installation Complies With Dbi Requirement is OFF CALENDAR. On September 24, 2021 this Court granted Defendant's second request for an order shortening time and ordered the defendant to provide Courtesy Copies of all filings the Court would consider for the hearing: moving papers, Opposition and Reply. Courtesy copies were to be delivered with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date by noon on September 27, 2021. Defendant delivered only courtesy copies of the moving papers. Defendant failed to comply with September 24, 2021 ex-parte order: No courtesy copies of opposition and reply delivered to the Court for September 29, 2021 hearing as of 12:14 p.m. on September 28, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES REID VS. JOHN H. MARBLE JR. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF JAMES REID MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden to show that there are no triable issues of fact and that Plaintiff is entitled to adjudication as a matter of law. Triable issue of fact exists whether Defendant is in possession pursuant to the sublease or per a direct agreement with the landlord. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that the validity of the agreement between the Defendant and the landlord can be determined in this unlawful detainer action and in the absence of the landlord. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
